intro: Ifloga paronychioides, also known as Ifloga, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. Its leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, providing relief from various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Ifloga paronychioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Ifloga include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to treat minor wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues.
